verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: “Nevertheless among the chief rulers also many believed on Him; but because of the Pharisees they did not confess Him, lest they should be put out of the synagogue: for they loved the praise of men more than the praise of God.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: It is necessary for us to avoid alike all the passions which corrupt the soul, but most especially those, which from themselves generate numerous sins.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: I mean such as the love of money.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: It is in truth of itself a dreadful malady, but it becomes much more grievous, because it is the root and mother of all mischiefs.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Such also is vainglory.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: See, for instance, how these men were broken off from the faith through their love of honor.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: “Many,” it saith, “of the chief rulers also believed on Him, but because of the Jews they did not confess Him, lest they should be put out of the synagogue.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: As He said also to them before, “How can ye believe which receive honor one of another, and seek not the honor that cometh from God only?” ( c. v.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: So then they were not rulers, but slaves in the utmost slavery.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: However, this fear was afterwards done away, for nowhere during the time of the Apostles do we find them possessed by this feeling, since in their time both rulers and priests believed.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: The grace of the Spirit having come, made them all firmer than adamant.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Since therefore this was what hindered them from believing at this time, hear what He saith.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: “He that believeth on Me, believeth not on Me, but on Him that sent Me.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: As though He had said, “Why fear ye to believe on Me?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Faith passeth to the Father through Me, as doth also unbelief.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: See how in every way He showeth the unvaryingness of His Essence.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: He said not, He that believeth “Me,” lest any should assert that He spake concerning His words; this might have been said in the case of mere men, for he that believeth the Apostles, believeth not them, but God.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: But that thou mightest learn that He speaketh here of the belief on His Essence, He said not, “He that believeth My words,” but, “He that believeth on Me.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: “And wherefore,” saith some one, “hath He nowhere said conversely, He that believeth on the Father, believeth not on the Father but on Me?”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Because they would have replied, “Lo, we believe on the Father, but we believe not on thee.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Their disposition was as yet too infirm.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Anyhow, conversing with the disciples, He did speak thus: “Ye believe on the Father, believe also on Me” ( c. xiv.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: 1 ); but seeing that these then were too weak to hear such words, He leadeth them in another way, showing that it is not possible to believe on the Father, without believing on Him.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: And that thou mayest not deem that the words are spoken as of man, He addeth,

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: “He that seeth Me, seeth Him that sent Me.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Is God a body?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: By no means.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: The “seeing” of which He here speaketh is that of the mind, thence showing the Consubstantiality.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: And what is, “He that believeth on Me”?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: It is as though one should say, “He that taketh water from the river, taketh it not from the river but from the fountain”; or rather this image is too weak, when compared with the matter before us.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: “I am come a light into the world.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: For since the Father is called by this name everywhere both in the Old (Testament) and in the New, Christ useth the same name also; therefore Paul also calleth Him, “Brightness” ( Heb. i.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: 3 ), having learnt to do so from this source.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: And He showeth here His close relationship with the Father, and that there is no separation between them, if so be that He saith that faith on Him is not on Him, but passeth on to the Father.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: And He called Himself “light,” because He delivereth from error, and dissolveth mental darkness.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: “If any man hear not Me, and believe not, I judge him not, for I came not to judge the world, but to save the world.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: For lest they should think, that for want of power He passed by the despisers, therefore spake He the, “I came not to judge the world.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Then, in order that they might not in this way be made more negligent, when they had learned that “he that believeth is saved, and he that disbelieveth is punished,” see how He hath also set before them a fearful court of judgment, by going on to say,

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: “He that rejecteth Me, and receiveth not My words, hath One to judge him.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: “If the Father judgeth no man, and thou art not come to judge the world, who judgeth him?”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: “The word that I have spoken, the same shall judge him.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: For since they said, “He is not from God,” He saith this, that, “they shall not then be able to say these things, but the words which I have spoken now, shall be in place of an accuser, convicting them, and cutting off all excuse.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: “And the word which I have spoken.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: What manner of word?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: “For I have not spoken of Myself, but the Father which sent Me, He gave Me a commandment what I should say, and what I should speak.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: And other such like.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Surely these things were said for their sakes, that they might have no pretense of excuse.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Since if this were not the case, what shall He have more than Isaiah? for he too saith the very same thing, “The Lord God giveth me the tongue of the learned, that I should know when I ought to speak a word.” ( Isa. l.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: What more than Jeremiah? for he too when he was sent was inspired. ( Jer. i.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: What then Ezekiel? for he too, after eating the roll, so spake. ( Ezek. iii.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Otherwise also, they who were about to hear what He said shall be found to be causes of His knowledge.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: For if when He was sent, He then received commandment what He should say, thou wilt then argue that before He was sent He knew not.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: And what more impious than these assertions? if (that is) one take the words of Christ in this sense, and understand not the cause of their lowliness?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Yet Paul saith, that both he and those who were made disciples knew “what was that good and acceptable and perfect will of God” ( Rom. xii.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: 2 ), and did the Son not know until He had received commandment?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: How can this be reasonable?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Seest thou not that He bringeth His expressions to an excess of humility, that He may both draw those men over, and silence those who should come after.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: This is why He uttereth words befitting a mere man, that even so He may force us to fly the meanness of the sayings, as being conscious that the words belong not to His Nature, but are suited to the infirmity of the hearers.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: “And I know that His commandment is life everlasting; whatsoever I speak therefore, even as the Father said unto Me, so I speak.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Seest thou the humility of the words?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: For he that hath received a commandment is not his own master.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Yet He saith, “As the Father raiseth up the dead and quickeneth them, even so the Son quickeneth whom He will.” ( c. v.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Hath He then power to quicken whom He will, and to say what He will hath He not power?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: What He intendeth then by the words is this; “The action hath not natural possibility, that He should speak one set of words, and I should utter another.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: “And I know that His commandment is life everlasting.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: He said this to those that called Him a deceiver, and asserted that He had come to do hurt.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: But when He saith, “I judge not,” He showeth that He is not the cause of the perdition of these men.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: By this He all but plainly testifies, when about to remove from, and to be no more with, them, that “I converse with you, speaking nothing as of Myself, but all as from the Father.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: And for this cause He confined His discourse to them to humble expressions, that He might say, “Even until the end did I utter this, My last word, to them.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: What word was that?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: “As the Father said unto Me, so I speak.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: “Had I been opposed to God I should have said the contrary, that I speak nothing of what is pleasing to God, so as to attract the honor to Myself, but now I have so referred all things to Him, as to call nothing My own.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Why then do ye not believe Me when I say that ‘I have received a commandment,’ and when I so vehemently remove your evil suspicion respecting rivalry?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: For as it is impossible for those who have received a commandment to do or say anything but what their senders wish, as long as they fulfill the commandment, and do not forge anything; so neither is it possible for Me to say or do anything except as My Father willeth.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: For what I do He doeth, because He is with Me, and ‘the Father hath not left Me alone.’” ( c. viii.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Seest thou how everywhere He showeth Himself connected with Him who begat Him, and that there is no separation?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: For when He saith, “I am not come of Myself,” He saith it not, as depriving Himself of power, but as taking away all alienation or opposition.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: For if men are masters of themselves, much more the Only-begotten Son.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: And to show that this is true, hear what Paul saith, “He emptied Himself, and gave Himself for us.” ( Philip. ii.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: But, as I said, a terrible thing is vainglory, very terrible ( Eph. v.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: 2 ); for this made these men not to believe, and others to believe ill, so that the things which were said for the sake of those men, through lovingkindness, they turned to impiety.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Let us then ever flee this monster: various and manifold it is, and everywhere sheds its peculiar venom, in wealth, in luxury, in beauty of person.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Through this we everywhere go beyond needful use; through this arises extravagance in garments, and a great swarm of domestics; through this the needful use is every where despised, in our houses, our garments, our table; and extravagance prevails.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Wilt thou enjoy glory?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Do alms-deeds, then shall Angels praise thee, then shall God receive thee.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Now the admiration goes no farther than the goldsmiths and weavers, and thou departest without a crown, often seeing that thou receivest curses.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: But if thou put not these things about thy body, but expend them in feeding the poor, great will be the applause from all sides, great the praise.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Then shalt thou have them, when thou givest them to others; when thou keepest them to thyself, then thou hast them not.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: For a house is a faithless treasury, but a sure treasury are the hands of the poor.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Why adornest thou thy body, while thy soul is neglected, possessed by uncleanness?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Why bestowest thou not so much thought on thy soul, as thy body?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Thou oughtest to bestow greater; but anyhow, beloved, we ought to bestow equal care upon it.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: For tell me, if any one asked thee which thou wouldest choose, that thy body should be fresh and of good habit and surpassing in beauty, and wear mean raiment, or having the body deformed and full of diseases, to wear gold and finery; wouldest thou not much prefer to have beauty depending on the nature of thy person, than on the raiment with which thou art clothed?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: And wilt thou choose this in the case of thy body, but the contrary in the case of thy soul; and, when thou hast that ugly and unsightly and black, dost thou think to gain anything from golden ornaments?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: What madness is this!

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Shift this adorning within, put these necklaces about thy soul.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: The things that are put about thy body help neither to its health nor to its beauty, for it will not make black white, nor what is ugly either beautiful or good looking.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: But if thou put them about thy soul, thou shalt soon make it white instead of black, instead of ugly and unsightly, thou shalt make it beautiful and well-favored.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: The words are not mine, but those of the Lord Himself, who saith, “Though thy sins be as scarlet, I will make them white as snow” ( Isa. i.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: 18 , LXX.); and, “Give alms—and all things shall be clean unto you” ( Luke xi.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: 41 ); and by such a disposition thou shalt beautify not thyself only, but thy husband.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: For they if they see you putting off these outward ornaments, will have no great need of expense, and not having it, they will abstain from all covetousness, and will be more inclined to give alms, and ye too will be able boldly to give them fitting counsel.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: At present ye are deprived of all such authority.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: For with what mouth will ye speak of these things? with what eyes will ye look your husbands in the face, asking money for alms, when ye spend most upon the covering of your bodies?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Then wilt thou be able boldly to speak with thy husband concerning alms-giving, when thou layest aside thine ornaments of gold.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Even if thou accomplish nothing, thou hast fulfilled all thy part; but I should rather say, that it is impossible that the wife should not gain the husband, when she speaks by the very actions.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: “For what knowest thou, O woman, whether thou shalt save thy husband?” ( 1 Cor. vii.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: As then now thou shalt give account both for thyself and for him, so if thou put off all this vanity thou shalt have a double crown, wearing thy crown and triumphing with thy husband through those unalloyed ages, and enjoying the everlasting good things, which may we all obtain, through the grace and lovingkindness of our Lord Jesus Christ, to whom be glory for ever and ever.
